_comment: Enterprise AI risk tiers — the decision-authority ladder. A system's tier determines its permitted autonomy, who may approve promotion to production, what evidence is required, how often it must be revalidated, and the mandatory fallback. Controls (controls.json) reference these tier ids in applicability.risk_tiers. Checked fail-closed by control_register.py.

tiers.example: Internal text assistant, no sensitive data, no actions
tiers.permitted_autonomy: Free suggestion; output is advisory text only
tiers.fallback: Disable feature; manual work continues unaffected

tiers.permitted_autonomy: Read-only recommendation with citations; no actions
tiers.fallback: Fall back to searchable documentation and human process

tiers.example: Agent with limited tools; drafts only
tiers.permitted_autonomy: Prepares drafts (e.g. work-order draft); NO irreversible actions
tiers.fallback: Suppress drafting; human authors the artifact

tiers.example: Can affect operations, finance, customers, safety or OT
tiers.permitted_autonomy: May act only within policy; high-impact actions require human approval
tiers.approval_authority: Cross-functional review (AI Governance Lead + Security + Legal + Process owner)
tiers.fallback: Degraded mode: advisory-only, no irreversible actions; then manual

tiers.example: Production changes, safety-critical or regulatory decisions
tiers.permitted_autonomy: No autonomous action; human control with dedicated approval for every action
tiers.approval_authority: AI Architecture Council + accountable executive; recorded per action
tiers.fallback: Fail-closed to manual; no autonomous path exists

kill_switch.effect: Immediately disables the AI action path (PDP denies all); advisory/manual modes may remain
kill_switch.process: Trigger -> PDP global-deny flag -> incident opened -> post-incident review before re-enable
